relatedness_reasoning: Both files concern account risk and compliance actions; inbound arrears_flags triggers outbound account freeze/block decisions. Risk_and_compliance is both consumer of inbound and provider of outbound, forming a logical pipeline step where arrears severity and risk category drive freeze actions.

mappings.reasoning: System-generated unique identifier for message exchange; no source in inbound schema.
Both are ISO8601 timestamps; outbound timestamp likely derived from or correlated with inbound flag_date when freeze action is initiated.
Inbound metadata.destination_department is 'Risk_and_Compliance', which becomes the source system for the outbound message.
Outbound destination 'accounts' is a routing decision not present in inbound schema.
Message type 'account_freeze_or_block' is a classification of the outbound message intent, not derived from inbound fields.
Inbound flag_severity (MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL) maps to outbound priority; CRITICAL severity would trigger high priority freeze message.
When compliance_action_required is true and risk_category indicates DEFAULT_RISK, freeze action is triggered; mapping is conditional rather than direct.
Inbound account_id (account_number format) becomes outbound accountNumber (IBAN format); likely reformatted to IBAN standard.
Inbound customer_name directly maps to outbound accountHolder; same semantic meaning, different field name.
Inbound product_type (Mortgage/Personal Loan/Credit Card) differs from outbound accountType enum (Current Account); weak mapping, likely requires business logic.
Outbound currency is hardcoded to GBP; inbound schema has no currency field to source this from.
Outbound branch is hardcoded to 'London City'; inbound schema contains no branch information.
Freeze type 'full' is a business decision not derivable from inbound arrears data alone.
Inbound risk_category (DEFAULT_RISK/WATCH_LIST/EARLY_WARNING) could trigger freeze, but outbound reason is 'suspected_fraud' which is not explicitly in inbound; weak mapping.
Narrative description likely constructed from inbound notes, arrears_status, and outstanding_balance to explain freeze rationale.
Name of person initiating freeze is not present in inbound schema; system-generated or from separate user context.
Outbound department 'Fraud Investigation Unit' is hardcoded; inbound has no equivalent department field.
Freeze effective date likely set to or derived from inbound flag_date when freeze decision is made.
Transaction restriction flags are business policy decisions not derivable from inbound arrears data.

Escalation level could be derived from flag_severity (CRITICAL=higher level); mapping is indirect and conditional.
Approval requirement likely correlates with inbound compliance_action_required flag.
Approval deadline is a business process decision not present in inbound schema.
Assigned compliance officer name is not derivable from inbound arrears data.
Case reference identifier is system-generated or from external case management system, not in inbound schema.
Risk score could be computed from inbound flag_severity, risk_category, and arrears_days; mapping is derived rather than direct.
Notification status is a process state not present in inbound schema; determined by downstream systems.
Regulatory reporting requirement could be triggered by CRITICAL severity or DEFAULT_RISK category, but mapping is conditional business logic.
